mechanism_narrative: Persistently low federal funds rates reduce the discount rate for equity valuations, generating positive forward returns for the S&P 500 over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid three-month increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and weakening consumer demand, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in >65% of triggered instances over a 5-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts confirms broad-based consumer demand strength, which sustains corporate earnings growth and produces positive S&P 500 returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive signals yield negative forward returns in >55% of cases, or interaction term loses statistical significance (p > 0.10) in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production reflects expanding manufacturing output and supply-side momentum, which translates into positive short-term S&P 500 returns within five trading days.
failure_condition: Signal direction reverses during tightening cycles, or 5-day forward returns show no significant correlation (t-stat < 1.5) with INDPRO growth.

mechanism_narrative: Elevated policy rates combined with decelerating nonfarm payrolls indicate monetary tightening is suppressing economic activity, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over one month.
failure_condition: The joint condition fails to predict negative returns (accuracy < 50%) or the interaction effect is statistically indistinguishable from zero.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth exceeding the 2.0% quarterly threshold confirms robust aggregate demand, which supports corporate profit margins and drives positive S&P 500 returns over a three-month horizon.
failure_condition: Forward returns are negative in >60% of threshold-crossing periods, or the signal loses predictive power (Rank IC < 0.04) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation erodes real corporate earnings and forces higher discount rates, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns within five trading days.
failure_condition: Positive CPIAUCSL growth consistently coincides with positive 5-day equity returns, or the correlation coefficient exceeds +0.10 over a 10-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent increases in total nonfarm payrolls and decreases in the unemployment rate signal a strengthening labor market that boosts consumer spending and yields positive S&P 500 returns over one month.
failure_condition: The dual labor signal predicts negative returns in >50% of instances during recovery phases, or the combined effect is statistically insignificant.
